steps.title: Resolve the surprising result
steps.content: The passage sets up a surprising contrast. Three-host complex life cycle parasites are usually shielded during transmission because they move through ingestion, while directly transmitted parasites are exposed to external conditions. Even so, Hart and colleagues found that three-host parasite abundance decreased as sea temperatures rose, whereas the abundance of directly transmitted parasites remained stable.

steps.title: Reject unsupported mechanisms
steps.content: Choice B invents a comparison among complex life cycle parasites that the passage never makes. Choice C contradicts the observed decline by claiming that more host species give better protection from rising temperatures. Choice D contradicts the passage because directly transmitted parasites are described as exposed, not shielded. The result is best explained by saying the shielding advantage existed but did not fully overcome other warming-related harms. The answer is A.
